title: Anthropic Cybersecurity Skills
description: A guided tour of the largest open-source collection of 734 AI-ready cybersecurity skills, covering threat hunting, forensics, cloud security, and more — mapped to MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF, and OWASP frameworks.

steps.title: Welcome to the Cybersecurity Skills Library
steps.description: This repository contains 734 structured cybersecurity skills compatible with Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and 20+ AI platforms. Skills span 36 subdomains including threat hunting, malware analysis, cloud security, and incident response. Let's explore how it's organized.

steps.title: The Skills Collection
steps.description: The skills/ directory is the heart of the project — 734 individual skill directories, each following the agentskills.io standard. Every skill contains a SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter for efficient AI discovery and a detailed Markdown body with practical, step-by-step workflows using real tools and commands.

steps.title: Anatomy of a Skill
steps.description: Each skill can include: SKILL.md (required — overview, prerequisites, practical steps, validation criteria), references/ (standards and deep documentation), scripts/ (agent.py for AI automation, process.py for practitioners), and assets/ (report templates and checklists). The YAML frontmatter enables token-efficient discovery — AI agents read approximately 30-50 tokens to decide relevance before loading the full body.

steps.title: Framework Mappings
steps.description: The mappings/ directory aligns all 734 skills to major cybersecurity frameworks. This includes a MITRE ATT&CK Navigator layer covering 218 techniques across all 14 tactics, NIST CSF 2.0 alignment across all 6 core functions (Govern, Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover), and OWASP Top 10 coverage. These mappings enable gap analysis and compliance alignment.

steps.title: AI Platform Integration
steps.description: The .claude-plugin/ directory contains plugin.json and marketplace.json for native Claude Code integration via the /plugin command. The index.json at the root is an auto-generated searchable index of all 734 skills, enabling AI agents to efficiently discover and load relevant skills for any cybersecurity task.

steps.title: CI/CD & Quality Automation
steps.description: GitHub Actions workflows in github/workflows/ automatically validate skill structure (YAML frontmatter, directory conventions) and regenerate index.json on every push. Issue templates in github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ standardize contributions for bug reports, new skill requests, and improvements — ensuring consistent quality across all 734 skills.

steps.title: Community & Governance
steps.description: The project maintains high standards through CONTRIBUTING.md (skill naming conventions, quality checklist, subdomain list), SECURITY.md (vulnerability reporting with 48-hour response SLA), CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (Contributor Covenant), and Apache 2.0 licensing. This open governance model has enabled a community-driven library of 734 production-ready cybersecurity skills.

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description: You've now seen the full architecture: 734 cybersecurity skills organized by subdomain, mapped to MITRE ATT&CK (218 techniques), NIST CSF 2.0, and OWASP frameworks, with native AI platform integration and automated quality enforcement. Whether you're an AI agent executing security workflows or a practitioner building detection capabilities, this library provides actionable, validated skills across the entire cybersecurity landscape.
